title: Fixed vs. Growth Mindset
author: From: Mindset
content: A fixed mindset believes ability is static. A growth mindset believes ability can be developed. Growth is the only path to mastery.
action: Action: Reframe a recent failure as a skill-acquisition opportunity.

title: The Margin of Safety
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: In life and strategy, always build in a buffer. If you operate at the absolute edge of your capacity, a single surprise will destroy your plan.
action: Action: Add a 20% buffer to your next project timeline.

title: The Map is Not the Territory
author: From: Thinking in Systems
content: Your model of reality is not reality itself. Always leave room for the model to be wrong.
action: Action: Identify one assumption you have that might be based on a flawed model.

title: Second-Order Thinking
author: From: The Art of Thinking Clearly
content: Don't just ask 'and then what?'. Ask 'and then what after that?'. Poor decision-makers only see the immediate consequence.
action: Action: Trace the consequences of your next decision three steps forward.

title: The Law of Diminishing Returns
author: From: Principles
content: More input does not always equal more output. Know when to stop adding effort to a project that has hit its peak utility.
action: Action: Stop work on a task that has reached its point of diminishing returns.
